name: Codicillary Rules of Engagements for the Flaming Fist
author: Flaming Fist High Command
description:
The Flaming Fist's formal rules of engagement for mercenary contracts — escalation ladders, prisoner treatment, and when an officer may refuse an employer's order without court-martial.

Outsiders find the codicil dry; soldiers in the Outer City treat selected passages as scripture.

name: Corsairs of Luskan
author: Unknown
description:
Corsairs of Luskan — Military history and counsel from campaigns fought along the Frontier and the Sword Coast. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Veterans annotate the margins with corrections; green officers treat those notes as insults until the first ambush.

name: Cookbook of the Realms
author: Unknown
description:
Cookbook of the Realms — Commercial wisdom dressed as prose — prices, routes, guild customs, and when to walk away from a deal. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to 15th century DR.

Factors along the Long Road keep folded excerpts in strongboxes next to their scales and seals.

name: Chronicles of Rothilion
author: Rothilion the Honorable
description:
Chronicles of Rothilion — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Rothilion the Honorable is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 714 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Crown Book of the Realm of Cormyr
author: Various
description:
Crown Book of the Realm of Cormyr — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Various is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to continually updated.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Cormyrian Customs And Scandals
author: Asgerlan Burtrann
description:
Cormyrian Customs And Scandals — A text that names names — or appears to — and has outlived more than one owner. Asgerlan Burtrann is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to unknown.

Several entries end mid-sentence, as if the author learned something and chose discretion over royalties.
